scenarios.name: 2000s Casual
scenarios.description: Let's put together a casual 2000s look. Let's take inspiration from the sports trends of the era!

incorrectSlotFeedback.top: The 2000s embraced boho styles, consider experimenting with fun colors and flowy shapes!
incorrectSlotFeedback.bottoms: Miniskirts had a resurgence in 2000s, often in fabrics like denim.
incorrectSlotFeedback.shoes: Wedge heels were a popular choice in the 2000s, for both dressy and casual looks.

top.commentary: Perfect! Bright colors and flowy fabrics fit in perfectly with boho trends of the 2000s.

bottoms.commentary: That’s right! Miniskirts were a popular casual choice in the 2000s.

shoes.commentary: Great work! Wedge heels and ankle straps were popular design choices in the 2000s for casual footwear.

scenarios.name: 2000s Formal
scenarios.description: Style a formal look for the 2000s, like getting dinner at a fancy restaurant!

scenarios.name: 2000s Business
scenarios.description: Let’s style a business look! How about a “casual” Friday at the office?

incorrectSlotFeedback.jacket: Y2K club outerwear was about casual luxury — think velour track jackets, cropped jackets, or fur-trimmed boleros.
incorrectSlotFeedback.top: The early 2000s club demanded maximum impact: halter tops, tube tops, sequins, metallics, and lots of exposed skin.
incorrectSlotFeedback.bottoms: Low-rise was non-negotiable in 2003. Bootcut or flared jeans, or a mini skirt, completed the look.
incorrectSlotFeedback.shoes: Strappy heels, pointed-toe boots, or platform sandals were the club footwear of choice in the early 2000s.

jacket.commentary: Juicy Couture velour became a status symbol. The track jacket was worn to the club and taken off at the door — casual luxury personified.

top.commentary: The halter top was the centerpiece of 2000s going-out fashion. Sparkle was mandatory, subtlety was not.

bottoms.commentary: Low-rise jeans defined Y2K fashion. The lower the waistband, the more on-trend you were. Fashion historians still debate how this happened.

shoes.commentary: Platform shoes bridged the late 90s and early 2000s. For a more authentic 2003 look, strappy heeled sandals would be perfect.

scenarios.name: 1980s Formal
scenarios.description: Let’s put together a formal look inspired by the 1980s! Give me your best red-carpet look!

top.commentary: The longline tee embodied 2015's streetwear-casual aesthetic. Extended hems and curved cuts were everywhere, from Coachella to city streets.

bottoms.commentary: An oversized blazer is very 2010s but more suited to a gallery opening than a dusty festival field.

shoes.commentary: The white sneaker trend peaked in the mid-2010s. Stan Smiths alone became a cultural phenomenon.

scenarios.name: 1980s Casual
scenarios.description: Style a 1980s casual outfit! Consider taking inspiration from hip-hop and fitness trends!

top.commentary: A graphic tee under the hoodie was standard layering. In 2001, the tees featured skate logos, video game references, or pop-punk bands.

bottoms.commentary: Cargo shorts represented early 2000s practicality-as-fashion. The more pockets, the better — even if you never used them.

shoes.commentary: Skate shoes with fat tongues and padded collars were non-negotiable. The brand on your feet was a social signal in middle school.

scenarios.name: 2010s Business
scenarios.description: Put together a 2010s business outfit. Try to capture the unique coolness of the 2010s!
